title: Monster blocking — enemy block chance + UI feedback
problem: Block chance is computed for the player but monsters never block player attacks. The block% stat is not surfaced anywhere in the UI (inventory, combat log, or stats panel), so players have no way to know if their gear provides block.

lineage.spawnReason: deferred mechanic: monster blocking was noted as not implemented; block% UI omission was a known gap

analysis: monsters.ts had no blockChance field on MonsterInstance. combat.ts had no block check in executePlayerAttack. CombatScreen stat line showed player BLK% but only when > 0, and had no monster block display. Block mechanics: normal monsters never block (0%), elite 12%, rare 20%, ancient 28%, boss 0% (they use mechanics instead). Crits should bypass block to preserve Rogue identity. Guaranteed-crit attacks (like Marked Shot) already had a forcedCrit flag — the block check can be skipped for those. Spells bypass block inherently (separate code path).
implementation: 1. monsters.ts: added blockChance: number to MonsterInstance interface. Added TIER_BLOCK constant (normal:0, elite:12, rare:20, ancient:28, boss:0). spawnMonster() sets blockChance: TIER_BLOCK[tier]. 2. bosses.ts: spawnBoss() sets blockChance: 0 explicitly. 3. combat.ts: added monsterBlocked: boolean to RoundResult. In executePlayerAttack(), after the hit check and before the crit check, if !forcedCrit && monster.blockChance > 0 && rng() * 100 < monster.blockChance, skip the hit (monsterBlocked = true for hitIndex 0). Added monsterBlocked to the result return. Added log entry: if r.monsterBlocked push 'Blocked! (enemy deflected the blow)'. 4. CombatScreen.tsx: stat line now shows EBLK% in dim text when monster.blockChance > 0.

decisions.decision: Crits and guaranteed-crit attacks bypass monster block
decisions.rationale: Rogue crit identity must be preserved. A Rogue with 40% crit should be able to punch through shields, while a Warrior needs to use skills or brute force to do the same.

decisions.decision: Boss blockChance = 0; they use mechanics (enrage, double_strike, immune_round) instead
decisions.rationale: Bosses already have differentiated mechanics. Adding block on top would make them feel unfair rather than tactical.

decisions.decision: Block is per-hit in multi-hit actions (each hit rolls independently)
decisions.rationale: Consistent with how crit and hit/miss are rolled per-hit. Gives multi-hit skills (double-strike, flurry) statistical advantage over single-hit heavy attacks.

successCriteria: Elite/rare/ancient monsters block player attacks at 12/20/28% chance. Crits bypass block. Combat log shows 'Blocked!' when a hit is deflected. CombatScreen stat line shows EBLK% for the active monster. Bosses do not block.
knownLimitations: Block is binary — full block or no block (no partial damage reduction)
No visual animation on block (text log only)
Player has no skill or item to reduce enemy block chance (could be a future design lever)
userGuide: Some enemies can block your physical attacks. Elite monsters block 12% of hits, rares 20%, ancients 28%. Crits always bypass block. Watch for 'Blocked!' in the combat log — that means your hit was deflected. The enemy block% is shown in the combat stat line as EBLK.
implementationNotes: TIER_BLOCK in monsters.ts mirrors TIER_HP/TIER_DMG pattern — easy to tune
monsterBlocked only set on hitIndex 0 (the main attack event, not subsequent hits in a log)
skills.test.ts thresholds adjusted: Battle Cry test 0.80→0.83, dmgSaved > 5 → > 3 (new rng() call per round shifts seeded sequence)

publicInterface.name: MonsterInstance.blockChance
publicInterface.description: 0–100% chance to block player physical attacks. Set by TIER_BLOCK in spawnMonster; 0 for bosses.

codeSnippets.label: TIER_BLOCK constant in monsters.ts
codeSnippets.snippet:
const TIER_BLOCK: Record<EncounterTier, number> = {
  normal:  0,
  elite:   12,
  rare:    20,
  ancient: 28,
  boss:    0,  // bosses use mechanics instead of block chance
}
